channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump hosts Republican governors in Florida and talks anew about Greenland and drones
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump hosted 22 of the nation's 27 Republican governors for dinner at his Florida club on Thursday night, looking to help drive the agenda in conservative-run states after he takes office in 11 days. Montana Gov.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A sharply divided Supreme Court has rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Sen. J.D. Vance to resign ahead of vice presidential inauguration
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- U.S. Sen. J.D. Vance will resign from his seat in Congress' upper chamber on Thursday ahead of his inauguration as vice president to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Pennsylvania GOP Rep. Meuser considering run for governor
item.description: Rep. Dan Meuser (R-Pa.) signaled he is seriously considering challenging Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro (D) for his post in 2026. “I’m considering it,” Meuser told The Hill on Thursday. “I mean if I get in it’s because I think I can win and that means beating the incumbent which is Josh Shapiro.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Michelle Obama doesn't attend state funeral for Jimmy Carter
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama did not attend the state funeral Thursday for former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House passes ICC sanctions bill; GOP Senate leader promises to bring it to floor
item.description: The House on Thursday passed legislation to sanction officials with the International Criminal Court (ICC), pushing back against the court's decision to issue arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his former defense minister for their actions during Israel’s war against H
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some notable quotes from Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Jimmy Carter was remembered at his funeral Thursday at Washington National Cathedral as a man of “character” and “something of a miracle” with “prophetic” vision.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US orange juice and steel will be on Canada's list for retaliation if Trump imposes tariffs
item.description: A senior official says Canada is looking at putting retaliatory tariffs on American orange juice, sinks, toilets and some steel products if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump goes ahead with his threat to impose 25% on all Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A contentious push to wrap up the 9/11 prosecutions takes an emotional toll on victims' families
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After his only child was killed on 9/11, Ken Fairben looked for justice in a far-off military courtroom on the Guantánamo Bay naval base in Cuba. He traveled there multiple times to observe hearings for accused Sept.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams delivers 4th State of the City address
item.description: HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his fourth State of the City address at The Apollo in Harlem on Thursday. Adams discussed topics like public safety, the cost of living, and new initiatives to build more in the city.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Trump, Obama exchange pleasantries at Carter funeral after long-standing rivalry
item.description: President-elect Trump and former President Obama had an extended friendly exchange as they sat next to each other at the funeral services for former President Carter on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ohio Supreme Court hears arguments in watchdog group's records dispute with state attorney general
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The Ohio Supreme Court heard oral arguments Wednesday in a long-running public records case pitting the state’s top law enforcement officer against a national watchdog group that is digging into his ties with the Republican Attorneys General Association.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fani Willis seeks to overturn her disqualification from Trump Georgia election case
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis has asked Georgia's highest court to review a lower appeals court's ruling that removed her from the Georgia election interference case against Donald Trump and others.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A contentious push to wrap up the 9/11 prosecutions takes an emotional toll on victims' families
item.description: Sept. 11 survivors and victims’ relatives have mixed feelings about a planned plea deal for accused 9/11 mastermind Khalid Sheikh Mohammed.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Peltz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden cancels trip to Italy, meant as final foreign visit of presidency, as fires rage in California
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden canceled the final overseas trip of his presidency just hours before he was set to depart for Rome and the Vatican, choosing to remain in Washington to monitor the response to devastating fires raging in California.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New book alleges that someone at Fox News gave Trump's team early look at town hall questions
item.description: Someone inside Fox News gave Donald Trump's presidential campaign the questions he would face ahead of a town hall the network produced in January 2024, a forthcoming book alleges, according to excerpts reported by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan J. Cooper, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio's COVID-era health director becomes first Dem to file for governor
item.description: “I’m a doctor, not a politician,” she said in a statement announcing her candidacy. “I solve problems by bringing people together regardless of party to find solutions.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Garland to release Smith's Jan. 6 report, hold back Mar-a-Lago report
item.description: Attorney General Merrick Garland plans to release only the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s plans to subvert the transfer of power after his loss in the 2020 election, holding back on sharing the Mar-a-Lago report while the president-elect's two co-defendants
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Exxon sues California AG for defamation after he sued them for 'misleading' plastic recycling claims
item.description: A major oil company has responded to California Attorney General Rob Bonta with a lawsuit alleging defamation after he accused them of misleading customers.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, Fox 5

item.title: Judge grants media requests to record Minnesota senator’s burglary trial
item.description: Minnesota Sen. Nicole Mitchell is set to stand trial later this month on a burglary charge. A Becker County judge on Tuesday ruled that the proceedings can be recorded by media organizations with some limitations.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: US transfers 11 Guantanamo detainees to Oman after more than two decades without charge
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon said Monday it had transferred 11 Yemeni men to Oman this week after holding them for more than two decades without charge at the U.S. naval base in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmakers prepare to take office
item.description: The Minnesota House will enter the 2025 legislative session with a new power structure and also a new home base down the block as its typical digs undergo construction.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Biden creates 2 new national monuments, setting a conservation record
item.description: The White House says President Joe Biden has now protected a total of 674 million acres of lands and waters — a record for any administration. This includes two new national monuments in California.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly was in a 'Cabinet meeting' during Mar-a-lago visit
item.description: While Trump's approach is high-risk, O'Reilly argues it reflects his commitment to his agenda and disregard for political norms.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management will get new interim director
item.description: The interim director of the Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management, who has led the office since it was created, is stepping down.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: After Trudeau’s announcement Monday, Trump touted the idea that many Canadians would “LOVE” the idea of being the "51st State" of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: GOP sues over timing of special election crucial to Minnesota House power balance
item.description: A lawsuit challenges a decision by Gov. Tim Walz to schedule a Jan. 28 special election after a November winner was deemed ineligible to take office in the House on Jan. 14.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota session agendas start to emerge as power fight drags on, acrimony picks up
item.description: A week ahead of the kick off of the 2025 legislative session, House leaders feuded over who is in charge and what might get accomplished amid a power struggle.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: President-elect Trump's lawyers ask judge to halt sentencing in hush money case
item.description: Editor's note: The video above is from Jan. 3, 2025. NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump asked a judge Monday to halt this week’s sentencing in his hush money case while they appeal a ruling upholding the verdict.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: President Biden has blocked new offshore drilling for oil and gas in several parts of the country about two weeks before President-elect Trump takes office. Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Wisconsin’s Evers revives plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws
item.description: Wisconsin Gov. Tony Evers is reviving his plan to enable voters to repeal and create state laws, this time through the state budget.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lombardo's State of the State speech set for Jan. 15
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo will give his 2025 State of the State Address on Wednesday, Jan. 15, at 6 p.m., according to a Friday statement from the governor's office.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 23-year-old hopeful files to run for Tampa mayor
item.description: The next mayoral race is not until 2027. For Alan Henderson, he hopes that’s enough time for people to know his name and what he hopes to accomplish, if elected.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nathan Fletcher's accuser dismisses sexual assault allegations from lawsuit
item.description: The former Metropolitan Transit System employee who accused Nathan Fletcher of sexual misconduct in a bombshell lawsuit that propelled his resignation from office has walked back some of her most serious allegations in new court filings.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5
